summary:
The patient demonstrates hallmark signs of Parkinson's disease—including resting tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, and a shuffling gait—all pointing toward a diagnosis of idiopathic Parkinson's. Only amantadine, among the provided drugs, effectively relieves core Parkinsonian motor symptoms and possesses confirmed antiviral properties, making it the most appropriate choice given the requirement that the treatment must be an antiviral relieving these symptoms.  

(Note: Even if amantadine is less frequently considered purely an antiviral today, within this context—the presence of antiviral designation combined with therapeutic effect—it remains unique.)

This summary focuses exclusively on critical clinical findings from the case presentation and directly addresses why one particular therapy stands out relative to others by matching both diagnostic criteria and pharmacological function.
